item.title: Crest Nicholson shares slump as housebuilder slashes earnings forecast
item.description: Housebuilder says ‘macro-uncertainty’ fuelled by war in the Middle East will push up cost pressures and damage consumer confidence

item.title: Refinitiv to delete profiles of politicians’ grandchildren after lawsuit
item.description: Minors sued data provider owned by London Stock Exchange Group over listing of relatives of ‘politically exposed persons’

item.title: Apple’s next chief Ternus faces defining AI moment
item.description: Tim Cook’s replacement must lead iPhone-maker through industry shift

item.title: Miliband unveils move to delink UK gas and electricity prices
item.description: Energy secretary’s push comes as Iran war threatens Labour pledge to cut heating bills

item.title: Primark owner ABF confirms plan to demerge fashion chain
item.description: Break-up of conglomerate after 65 years paves way for Primark listing

item.title: Global clean power growth points to permanent shift, analysts say
item.description: Evidence grows of structural move away from fossil fuels to meet electricity demand

item.title: The big short meets private credit
item.description: Wall Street banks have started trading credit default swaps against private credit funds run by Blackstone, Apollo and Ares

item.title: EQT and Omers to lift funding for struggling broadband group to €5bn
item.description: Funding plan comes after investors failed to find new backer to inject cash into debt-laden German provider

item.title: Private credit funds strained as borrowing costs edge higher
item.description: Bond investors and banks are seeking greater premiums on new financing as a precaution before lending

item.title: Will social media addiction go the way of cigarettes?
item.description: Smoking among the rich has declined dramatically — and digital dependency could follow a similar pattern

item.title: Tackling energy security can no longer be put off
item.description: The Middle East war should accelerate a shift to renewable power sources

item.title: Babcock’s cheaper warships offer the Royal Navy hope
item.description: The UK defence company is building Type 31 frigates quickly at Rosyth to fill an awkward gap

item.title: Sotheby’s strikes $100mn debt deal with KKR backed by auction fees
item.description: Borrowing facility adds another layer of debt to auction house owned by billionaire Patrick Drahi

item.title: JPMorgan to invest in Europe and UK in expanded $1.5tn security initiative
item.description: Bank’s 10-year plan will help companies in defence, energy and other critical sectors raise funding

item.title: Trump’s ‘Board of Peace’ held talks with DP World over Gaza reconstruction
item.description: Proposal examined having Emirati company manage supply chains and logistics as part of US plan for devastated enclave

item.title: In the age of AI, recruiters need to rethink their jobs
item.description: The more upmarket the staffing service, the better the chances of weathering the change

item.title: Top US insurance regulator warns of risks from push into private markets
item.description: ‘Less appropriate’ investments have grown since arrival of Apollo and other private capital groups, warns watchdog

item.title: UK school meal providers warn against push into ‘the world of lentils’
item.description: Government wants healthier options on menu as 26% of children in England are overweight or living with obesity, according to NHS

item.title: ‘Kiss of death’: How the US killed a Swiss merchant bank
item.description: MBaer survived years of scrutiny at home — before Washington stepped in to seal its fate

item.title: MPs chastise Starmer over Mandelson scandal
item.description: UK prime minister accused of scapegoating officials over Peter Mandelson appointment

item.title: Japan lifts ban on lethal arms exports
item.description: Major shift from pacifist stance will allow defence contractors to target international markets

item.title: Bezos’s AI lab nears $38bn valuation in funding deal
item.description: Company code-named Project Prometheus is working on models for industrial applications

item.title: Amazon’s ambition to rival Starlink set back after Blue Origin rocket grounded
item.description: Federal Aviation Administration forces Jeff Bezos’s space venture to halt operations of flagship New Glenn

item.title: Anthropic and Amazon agree $100bn AI infrastructure deal
item.description: Start-up behind Claude tool seeks to bulk up on chips and computing power after suffering outages this year

item.title: FirstFT: Apple CEO Tim Cook will step down in September
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: China-Japan tensions grow, and Keir Starmer fends off calls to quit

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to hand over to John Ternus in September
item.description: Head of hardware to become Apple’s next chief executive while Cook will become iPhone maker’s chair

item.title: The M&A strategy behind Berkshire Hathaway’s tie-up with Tokio Marine
item.description: Deal with non-life insurer is first investment in Japan beyond trading houses for Warren Buffett’s conglomerate

item.title: What does AI really mean for your work? Submit your questions
item.description: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with Sarah O’Connor, John Burn Murdoch and Madhumita Murgia on Thursday April 23 at 1pm BST

item.title: Heathrow can raise airline fees to pay for third runway bid, regulator says
item.description: Civil Aviation Authority’s draft decision comes despite criticism from carriers over airport’s expansion project

item.title: Trump’s call to deregulate psychedelics boosts drugmakers’ shares
item.description: Stock in UK-based Compass Pathways jumps to highest level in two years following president’s executive order
